title: Harness workflow envelope (the ONE shared receipt)
description: Short receipt every workflow round emits next to its payload. The orchestrator reads the envelope (cheap) not the payload (expensive); content_sha256 binds envelope->payload so drift is detectable; next_recommended_workflow drives chaining. Validate with engine/workflows/_shared/validate-envelope.py.

workflow.description: emitting workflow name (must be an engine/workflows/<name>/)

artifact_type.description: what the payload is: risk-assessment | bug-intake | reproduction | rca | fix-plan | fix-implementation | fix-verification | design | plan | task-implementation | validation

artifact_path.description: path to the payload this envelope receipts

status.description: workflow lifecycle/terminal state (see each workflow's State section)

verdict.description: workflow-specific verdict, e.g. SAFE_TO_IMPLEMENT | REQUIRES_CODEX_REVIEW | REQUIRES_BA_DECISION | BLOCKED_INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT | REPRODUCED | DIRECT_PATCH | PLAN_MISMATCH

summary_for_router.description: one to three sentences the orchestrator can act on WITHOUT reading the payload

next_recommended_workflow.description: the workflow this result should chain into, e.g. impact-analysis | technical-design | task-slicing | incremental-impl | bug-fix | progressive-test | deep-review, or null when terminal

content_sha256.description: sha256 hex of the payload file bytes; rerun validate-envelope --payload to detect drift

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp set by the writer
